structured.title: School Poetry Event With Children Performing
structured.overview: The recording captures a school poetry performance event where children present spoken word pieces before a large audience of parents and teachers. A teacher (likely Mr. Peter) welcomes everyone, notes the strong turnout, and explains that families may record their own child’s performance but must not upload videos to social media. They mention that individual performance videos are being recorded and will be shared later via a QR code. The organizer proposes a group photo at the end with parents and children in front of a banner. They also direct attendees to teas and coffees at the back and offer help with transport or lift arrangements. The performances begin with a student named Erin reading a poem about spending a day outside, experiencing minor mishaps, and ending on a positive note when the weather improves and a rainbow appears.

action_items.description: Ensure phone is set to silent mode

apps_results.content:
## Event setup and guidelines  
- Children performing spoken word/poetry for an audience  
- Filming allowed for own child but not social media  
- QR codes planned to share individual performance videos  

## Audience and logistics  
- Large turnout of parents and children present  
- Group photo planned with banner at the end  
- Teas, coffees available; help offered with lifts  

## Performances begin  
- Event handed over to student competitors  
- First performer introduced: Erin  
- Emphasis on building children’s spoken word skills  

## Erin’s poem: “A day outside”  
- Starts with bright sun and fun on roller skates  
- Picnic at the beach with family, choosing cola  
- Day worsens with falls, rain, bad mood, then rainbow  

## Next steps  
- Continue student performances after Erin  
- Group photo with parents and children at event end
